title: requests — error catalogue
description: Error codes this module emits. `user_facing` controls whether the message renders to end users (translated via i18n). `retryable` signals client-side retry. `audit_severity` routes to the correct log channel — `alert` pages on-call.

errors.description: The Google-Docs 403 hint. This is NOT an error emitted by THIS module — it's the CODE the `access.request_hint_on_denial` middleware writes into another module's 403 body. Documented here so the front-end error catalogue is complete.
The submitted `resource_type` is not in AccessRequestableRegistry.
The target resource exists but is in a different tenant. Cross-tenant access requests are forbidden. Warn severity because it may indicate an attempted lateral-move probe.
The `resource_id` does not resolve to a live row of `resource_type` in the caller's tenant.
One or more `permissions_wanted` are not declared on the resource type via `#[AccessRequestable(permissions: [...])]`.
`reason` must be between 20 and 1000 chars. Short reasons don't help approvers decide; over-long reasons are usually copy-paste artefacts.
The caller already has a pending request for the same (resource_type, resource_id, permissions_wanted) tuple. Withdraw or wait for the existing request before submitting a new one.
AccessRequestApproverResolver produced an empty approver set + resource-owner fallback also failed. Platform-side misconfiguration — pages on-call to fix the tenant's role assignments or template.
The caller is not in the resolved approvers set for any pending requirement on this request.
The caller already recorded an approve/reject decision on this request. Decisions are one-shot per approver.
The request is in a terminal state (approved / rejected / expired / cancelled). No decisions can be recorded.
Only pending requests can be withdrawn.
SOC 2 requires an auditable rationale on every reject. `comment` must be at least 10 characters.
The `access_requests` entitlement is FALSE for this tenant — opted out via ops-only setting for regulatory reasons.
`access_requests.max_per_month` pool exhausted for this tenant. Requires plan upgrade or resets on the next period.
Bulk approve is enterprise-only. Enterprise upgrade required.
Framework throttle tripped. Response includes X-RateLimit-Trigger + Retry-After.

AccessRequestGrantMaterializer raised while calling GrantIssuer::issue(). The approval_instance is left in status='approved' but `context_json.materialised_grant_id=NULL` — remediate via `access-requests:remediate-orphaned-approvals`. Pages on-call.
AccessRequestableRegistry has zero entries at request time. Indicates a boot-time discovery failure or a broken cache. Pages on-call.
